title: Cyathea perpunctulata
intro: Cyathea perpunctulata, also known as the spotted tree fern, is a species of tree fern native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used by the indigenous Māori people for its medicinal properties, which include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyathea perpunctulata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Māori medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and may also be made into a poultice or applied topically to the affected area.
